name: Meatless Chili with Corn Chips
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Low-sodium soy sauce (shoyu or tamari)
1/2 cup Minced mild chili peppers (usually anahiem)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the dried kidney beans in cold water to cover for 8 hours or overnight. Drain before using.
2. Combine crumbled tempeh, red wine, soy sauce, cumin, basil, and chili powder; let marinate for at least 2 hours.
3. In a large pot over medium-high heat, sauté onions in sherry and olive oil for 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
4. Add mushrooms and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
5. Stir in tomatoes, green peppers, carrots, celery, chili peppers, and jalapenos. Cook for 5 more minutes.
6. Add the soaked beans, vegetable stock, and tomato paste. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for 2-3 hours until beans are tender.
7. Return the tempeh and marinade to the pot; cook for an additional 10 minutes.
8. Serve garnished with corn chips.
NOTES: This recipe features a midwestern-style chili with beans and tomatoes. For a traditional approach, replace all tomatoes with a red chili sauce made from dried red anchos or other chilies, researced, and simmered in boiling water for 20 minutes before blending.
